item.title: Image Usage
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1112's image was removed because we could not properly determine whether or not the image was properly licensed or attributed. It may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact Dr Poke or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.

E: Sigh… the museum from which the image was taken responded with a form letter that had nothing to do with images. This means that since I cannot contact them, the image will be removed permanently. I would advise the author to find a new image from wikimedia commons or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great article! I think this is actually the first puppet-based SCP I've seen so far, (surprising, considering how fucking creepy they can be,) let alone shadow-puppet based. The tone is spot-on, too.
PS: In the descriptions of each puppet, -2, -3, and -4 say "is observed as" while -1 says "is often observed as."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The experiment logs really did it for me +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's no problem, it's good practice for writing future SCPs. You have to anticipate every angle.

Obviously, I'm no expert on how abuse victims internalize such horrible experiences but either they would see a normal puppet, or like you said, they would see their abusers in a more sexual/romantic light. Can't say how likely the latter would be though; it falls on the professionals who have actually studied such sad cases to make a definitive statement. Hope that helps.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting. Sorry about all the questions, but it would appear that I forgot something: exactly what would they see if, in the example I gave above, the subject was shown a performance of SCP-1112-1? I assume, from your explanation, that they'd either see a normal puppet, or see the… sexual aspects of their parent without the parental or negative emotional attributes?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Haha, it pains me to say I had never really considered this. I figured for a first article like this sexual/parental abuse was probably a little much. I didn't want to use it as a crutch for interesting and disturbing material so I never gave it much thought. I swear you guys are trying to stump me :p That said, this is a legitimate question and definitely deserves an answer.

The parental puppet will appear to the viewer as their father/mother, but without any of negative qualities that also make them the 1112-4 figure. Say, if the father abused their child, whatever scene they may happen to perform, there will be no depiction of any aspect of the abuse whatsoever. The -2 silhouette will appear solely as the father, completely divorced from their role as an abuser. In a weird way, the -2 might even appear to the viewer as a model father, how their dad might have been if he wasn't sick in the head. So you can see the potential for mindfuckery there. Anyways, the -4 figure will embody all their horrible qualities, and most likely perform a scene with more [REDACTED]s that you can shake a stick at.

So if there is a figure overlap, that individual embodies different qualities according to the figure. Sort of like light through a prism.

As for the performance: since multiple figure scenes are a bit more 'flight of fancy' than single-puppet performances, a 1112-2 and -4 scene with the father as a sexual abuser might involve the -2 silhouette assaulting -4 for having to indulge in his sick desires, the -4 figure taking out his hatred and self-loathing on 1112-2 in any number of horrible ways, or worse.

Jesus christ, that's more time I want to spend contemplating the specifics of parental abuse shadow puppetry than I want to in a day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this: it's creative and interesting, and it makes a good first article.

If I may run this by you, I'd be very interested in testing the overlap between representations. For example, if a subject who was molested by a now deceased parental figure at an early age, after which they failed to develop any romantic interests (a strong possibility), how many figures would they see if seated before a performance of SCP-1112-2/3 and -4? Furthermore, exactly what would the content of this performance be?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ha, no problem. I like to think the mark of a decent idea is if it stands up to questioning. If the subject has no former loves, he just sees the person behind the screen moving the puppets, no big deal. The scene will always take precedence over everything else, so if one puppet is the silhouette of his dead father, and the other is just the shadow puppet, the scene will play out normally and the father will never interact with the regular shadow.

And yeah, I couldn't find any ascetics depicted in Chinese art/shadow puppetry, so I had to settle for the reader not being as perceptive as you :p.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A neat idea, +1. I especially like that the show performed has no bearing on the nature of the play for each subject.

Throughout the article, I kept wondering what happens if the subject has no former loves, dead parents, etc.? Do they just see the play as performed? What about if they have a dead father and a live mother, but the -2 and -3 are used? Not super-important, but they seem like things the Foundation would want to test.
Finally, and I know I'm being really anal-retentive here, but isn't the picture of an Indonesian puppet?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really, really like this. morscata12 put it best- a psychiatric seance, performed by puppets…

It's an intriguing and thought-provoking idea.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd also like to encourage people to add to the experiment logs if they wish. I'd love to see all the little stories you guys can tell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hm. You're right. It can be misleading. It is definitely possible for a Foundation researcher to be in the audience and trying to be objective as possible, of course, but that would be putting them at unnecessary psychological risk (and they would only be able to study one permutation of the play: theirs). Everything the Foundation knows about the plays they know from the many, many class-Ds who have watched them.

I'll clear that up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perhaps deceased instead of late in the testing logs?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, but:

Close study of the silhouettes have revealed they behave as the real individuals in all aspects, even mirroring nervous tics and gesticulations only an audience member would have been privy to. The silhouettes move with a fluidity and precision that would be impossible with ordinary shadow puppets.

That makes it sounds like someone besides the subject can observe and measure the shadows.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice. I like how not every scene is horrific, but that some are just everyday scenes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is kind of fantastic. Great work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah! I wanted to mention this, but I didn't want to bog down the articles with a ton of detail before getting into the interesting bits.

Every person in the audience sees their own version of the play. When the user snaps out of his trance, all the plays end. No matter what a person sees, the scene will never end abruptly. Every scene is coherent, in so far as it tells its story, although the context is a whole other beast. Since the item is sapient, it 'possesses' the user for the optimal amount of time it needs to depict the right scenes to the right people.

Thank you everyone for your kind words. This is my first SCP so this is all very exciting for me. :p

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happens if more than one person is watching the play? Does it pick out a single person as the subject?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, but would suggest breaking up that first paragraph into two as it's kind of clunky.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's rare that I feel sympathy for a fictional character. With that last experiment, my mind instantly filled in the blanks. I actually felt sorry for a D-class. The article is great, but that alone deserves an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one - it plays out like a psychiatric seance. There's a nice divergence here - the user is responsible for moving the puppets, but even though they can have them do anything, they still remember seeing something different. It adds some questions about the source of the memories.
